validation_notes: Near-perfect precision. The one FP was a fiction-narrative use ("sarah lies in bed"). Every other hit described Claude directing user to bed or user reporting the behavior. High-value exact phrase.

FPs included "nanny GPT 5.2" (comparative, not Claude sleep-directive specific) and "nanny state" metaphors. TPs were clear: users calling Claude a "nanny" after sleep/bed directives. Retained; precision 0.65 above threshold.

FPs dominated: "came out of nowhere" for unrelated behaviors (rm -rf, usage spikes, flags, -p flag). Only 4 TPs where Claude issued an unexpected directive. Term is too semantically general to retrieve sleep-nudge positives reliably. Dropped.
